title: cerastium decalvans subsp. doerfleri
intro: Cerastium decalvans subsp. doerfleri, also known as Doerfler's mouse-ear or Doerfler's chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Cerastium decalvans subsp. doerfleri include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
